sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER, name STRING, event_date DATE, interval_days INTEGER);
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (1, 'apple', DATE '2023-01-01', 5), (2, 'banana', DATE '2023-01-05', NULL), (3, 'apply', DATE '2023-01-10', 10), (4, 'cherry', DATE '2023-01-15', 15);

SELECT id, name, event_date + INTERVAL (CASE WHEN interval_days IS NULL THEN 0 ELSE interval_days END) DAY AS updated_date FROM test_table WHERE starts_with(name, 'a') AND event_date < DATE '2023-01-10';
SELECT id, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY starts_with(name, 'a')) AS count_per_prefix FROM test_table WHERE updated_date = DATE '2023-01-06' + INTERVAL (0) DAY HAVING count_per_prefix > 0;

SELECT DISTINCT name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table AS t WHERE starts_with(t.name, LEFT(name, 1))) AS same_prefix_count FROM test_table WHERE event_date = (SELECT MAX(event_date) FROM test_table) ORDER BY same_prefix_count DESC;
SELECT id, name, event_date FROM test_table WHERE starts_with(name, 'a') ORDER BY event_date + INTERVAL (COALESCE(interval_days, 0)) DAY DESC;
CREATE TABLE temp_table AS SELECT id, interval_days, starts_with(name, 'b') AS has_b FROM test_table WHERE event_date + INTERVAL (COALESCE(interval_days, 0)) DAY < DATE '2023-01-16';
SELECT SUM(interval_days) OVER (ORDER BY id) AS cumulative_interval FROM temp_table;
ALTER TABLE test_table ADD COLUMN another_name STRING;
UPDATE test_table SET another_name = CONCAT(name, ' updated') WHERE starts_with(name, 'a');
SELECT id, another_name FROM test_table WHERE another_name IS NOT NULL AND id < 3 ORDER BY another_name + '';
